div1.entryFree: Prone, eagerly ready: that p. lust should stain so pure a bed, Lucr. 684. unless a man would marry a gallows and beget young gibbets, I never saw one so p. Cymb. V, 4, 208. With to: nor tender feeling, to base touches p. Sonn. 141, 6. I am not p. to weeping, Wint. II, 1, 108. as p. to mischief as able to perform 't, H8 I, 1, 160. Peculiar passage: in her youth there is a p. and speechless dialect, such as move men, Meas. I, 2, 188 (Nares: prompt, ready; Malone: significant, expressive. The words p. and speechless must be considered as a hendiadis, == speechlessly prone, prone even without speaking, speaking fervently and eagerly without words).
